title: 3 Opening and Operating an ICBC Agent芒聙聶s Account
body_text:
3	Opening and Operating an ICBC Agent芒聙聶s Account
3.1	Opening an ICBC Agent芒聙聶s Account
An ICBC agent can open an account on behalf of ICBC for deposit-taking purposes only. When an ICBC agent obtains approval from ICBC to open an account, ICBC provides the agent with an ICBC New Account Letter.
This letter includes the following information:
Authorization for the agent to deposit items payable to ICBC
Confirmation that Central 1 is the only party that is permitted to initiate withdrawals
Additional instructions for operating the account
Open an ICBC agent芒聙聶s account as follows:
Request and review the ICBC agent芒聙聶s ICBC New Account Letter. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: ICBC New Account Letter
Verify the ICBC agent芒聙聶s identity and follow internal procedures for opening accounts. If you芒聙聶re a subscriber to the Operations Manual Program, see the Business Accounts Part in The Guide (BC) for more information.
File the ICBC New Account Letter in the ICBC agent芒聙聶s member file along with the account documentation.
Once the ICBC agent芒聙聶s account is opened, the ICBC agent notifies ICBC, and ICBC notifies Central聽1 to activate the sweep. Your financial institution then begins forwarding monthly statements to ICBC as directed in the ICBC New Account Letter.
The ICBC agent芒聙聶s account must never be in overdraft. Service charges and misdirected Chargebacks (dishonoured cheques) must not be debited to the account.
3.2	Operating an ICBC Agent芒聙聶s Account
Only Central 1 is authorized to debit an ICBC agent芒聙聶s account. The account must never be in overdraft.
You can芒聙聶t debit misdirected chargebacks (dishonoured cheques) to the account. See Section 6.3, Managing Chargebacks for an ICBC Agent芒聙聶s Account, for more information.
You can芒聙聶t charge overdraft fees and/or service charges against the account. See Section 3.4, Reimbursement for an ICBC Agent Account芒聙聶s Overdraft Charges, or Section 3.5, Reimbursement for an ICBC Agent芒聙聶s Account Overdraft Charges, as appropriate.
3.3	ICBC Agent芒聙聶s Account Inquiries
ICBC agents芒聙聶 accounts are owned by ICBC. You must disclose account balances and other account information upon request to authorized individuals from ICBC. For security and privacy, contact Central聽1 Payment Services Specialists to confirm the names of authorized ICBC employees before providing account information. See Section 1.2, Contacts and Websites.
3.4	Reimbursement for an ICBC Agent芒聙聶s Account Overdraft Charges
Since ICBC agents芒聙聶 accounts are for deposits only, you can芒聙聶t debit an account for overdraft charges. Depending on the reason for the overdraft, you can seek reimbursement for overdraft charges as follows.
Errors made by an ICBC Agent
If an ICBC agent makes an error that results in overdraft charges, contact the agent for reimbursement. Don芒聙聶t debit the overdraft charges to the account.
Errors made by Central 1
If overdraft charges occur due to an error made by Central 1, don芒聙聶t debit the ICBC agent芒聙聶s account. Submit a Paper Payments and Cash Returns Trace Request in ServiceNow to request reimbursement from Central 1. See Section 6.2, Managing Deposit Errors for an ICBC Agent芒聙聶s Account, for instructions on creating a Paper Payments and Cash Returns Trace Request in ServiceNow.
Note:	Enter the reason for reimbursement request in the Detail Description section.
Central 1 will investigate and, if warranted, credit your Central 1 account for the overdraft charge. It will display on your Account Activity and Balances (AIBC) report as code 芒聙聹CM芒聙聺.
3.5	Reimbursement for an ICBC Agent芒聙聶s Account Service Charges
Since ICBC agent芒聙聶s accounts are deposit only, you can芒聙聶t debit the account for service charges (e.g., interest, statement fees, night depository charges). However, you can obtain reimbursement from ICBC by completing an ICBC service charge debit and processing it in Branch Capture. The ICBC service charge debit is MICR encoded by Central 1 with ICBC芒聙聶s Royal Bank account details.
Requesting MICR Encoded ICBC Service Charge Debits
To request a supply of 100 MICR encoded ICBC service charge debits:
Submit a Paper Payments and Cash Returns Trace Request in ServiceNow. See Section 6.2, Managing Deposit Errors for an ICBC Agent芒聙聶s Account, for instructions on creating a Paper Payments and Cash Returns Trace Request.
Provide the following information in the Detail Description field:
芒聢聮
Contact name
芒聢聮
Financial institution芒聙聶s name
芒聢聮
Branch transit
芒聢聮
Branch address
Central 1 will send the ICBC service charge debits to the branch address you provide in ServiceNow.
Completing and Processing ICBC Service Charge Debits in Branch Capture
To obtain reimbursement for ICBC agents芒聙聶 account service charges:
Complete an ICBC service charge debit, as follows. See Figure 2.
芒聭
Enter agent number as provided on the ICBC New Account Letter.
芒聭隆
Enter the current date.
芒聭垄
Enter the month for which the charges apply.
芒聭拢
Enter your financial institution芒聙聶s name.
芒聭陇
Enter the appropriate branch.
芒聭楼
Enter the total value of service charges associated with the account for the specified month.
Figure 2: Service Charge Debit Sample
Stamp the back of the ICBC service charge debit with your financial institution芒聙聶s identification Stamp.
Process the service charge debit in a Canadian deposit in Branch Capture. See the Deposit Processing Part for procedures on creating deposits in Branch Capture.
3.6	Changes to an ICBC Agent芒聙聶s Account Transit and Account Number
To advise Central 1 of changes to an ICBC agent芒聙聶s transit number or account number:
Complete ICBC Agent芒聙聶s Transit/Account Number Update, Form 2344. Submit the form at least one week before the effective date. Failure to advise Central 1 can cause processing delays and errors. Completion of this form is self-explanatory. See Figure 3.
Figure 3: ICBC Agent芒聙聶s Transit/Account Number Update, Form 2344
Forward the form as indicated. Keep a copy of the form for your records.
Central 1 will advise ICBC of the change.
END
